title: Image Compressor
description: Compress images in your browser — no server upload. Adjust quality and pick WebP, JPEG, or PNG output. Free tool.
dropZone: Click or drag & drop images here
accepts: JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF, BMP — multiple files supported

howToUse.label: Set format and quality
howToUse.description: Choose WebP, JPEG, or PNG as the output format. For WebP and JPEG, adjust the quality slider — around 80% gives a good balance of quality and file size.

howToUse.label: Upload images
howToUse.description: Drag and drop your images onto the upload area, or click to select files. Multiple files can be processed at once.

howToUse.label: Download compressed images
howToUse.description: The original size, compressed size, and reduction ratio are shown for each file. Click Download to save the result.

faq.question: How much can images be compressed?
faq.answer: At 80% quality, WebP and JPEG compression typically reduces file size by 50–80% compared to the original. PNG uses lossless compression, so Canvas-based conversion may not reduce the size much or may even increase it. For significant size reduction, choose WebP or JPEG.

faq.question: Should I use WebP or JPEG?
faq.answer: For web publishing, WebP is recommended — it achieves 20–30% smaller file sizes than JPEG at equivalent quality, and all modern browsers support it. For email attachments, social media, or situations where broad compatibility matters, JPEG is the safer choice.

faq.question: Are my images sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. All compression happens locally in your browser using the Canvas API. Your images are never sent to any external server.

faq.question: Does compression change the image dimensions?
faq.answer: No. This tool only applies quality-based compression — pixel dimensions remain unchanged. If you need to resize an image, use the Image Resizer tool.
